steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
The numerator starts with $-b = -9$, which eliminates choices A and B (they have $+9$).

Calculate the discriminant:
$$b^2 - 4ac = 9^2 - 4(1)(-13) = 81 + 52 = 133$$
Substituting into the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{-9 \pm \sqrt{133}}{2}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
